title: SmartPlaylist Definition
description: Defines how a smart playlist groups, filters, sorts, and displays episodes for a specific podcast. Each definition file lives at {patternId}/playlists/{id}.json. Multiple definitions under the same pattern share the podcast's episode pool and claim episodes through a priority-based system.

id.description: Unique identifier within the parent pattern. Must match the filename (without .json extension) in the playlists/ directory. Referenced by the pattern meta's playlists array to control display order.

displayName.description: Human-readable playlist name shown in the app UI. For rss, year, and titleAppearanceOrder resolvers, this may be overridden per-group at runtime by titleExtractor output. For the category resolver, group-level display names come from static GroupDef entries.

resolverType.description: Determines the algorithm used to group episodes into playlists. Each resolver interprets the episode data differently and supports different configuration fields.

oneOf.description: Groups episodes by their iTunes season number from RSS feed metadata (itunes:season tag). Each distinct season number becomes a separate group. Episodes without a season number are handled by nullSeasonGroupKey (assigned to a fallback group) or left ungrouped. Best for podcasts that properly tag seasons in their RSS feed. Supports: nullSeasonGroupKey, titleExtractor, smartPlaylistEpisodeExtractor.

Groups episodes by matching their titles against regex patterns defined in the groups array. Groups are evaluated in order; the first matching pattern claims each episode. A group without a pattern acts as a catch-all for unmatched episodes. Best for podcasts with naming conventions like '[Series A] Episode 1' or 'Arc 1: Title'. Requires: groups. Does not use titleExtractor (group display names come from GroupDef.displayName).

Groups episodes by their publication year (publishedAt field). Each distinct year becomes a separate group, sorted by year descending by default (newest first). Best for long-running podcasts where yearly grouping provides natural navigation. Supports: titleExtractor, smartPlaylistEpisodeExtractor.

Groups episodes by a recurring pattern extracted from their titles, ordered by when each unique pattern first appears in the feed (sorted by publication date). Uses titleExtractor or the first group's pattern to extract group names. Best for podcasts with titled story arcs or recurring series. Supports: titleExtractor (primary), groups[0].pattern (fallback for extraction).

priority.description: Controls episode claiming order among sibling definitions within the same pattern. Processing order: (1) definitions with at least one filter (titleFilter, excludeFilter, or requireFilter) are processed first, sorted by priority ascending; (2) definitions without any filters (fallbacks) are processed second, also sorted by priority ascending. Within each group, lower priority numbers claim matching episodes first. Once a filtered definition claims episodes, those episodes are unavailable to subsequent definitions.

contentType.description: Determines how resolver results are presented in the UI. When omitted, defaults to 'episodes'. In 'episodes' mode, each resolver result (season, year, category match) becomes a separate top-level smart playlist; tapping shows episodes directly. In 'groups' mode, all resolver results are collected as groups inside a single parent playlist; tapping the playlist shows group cards, and tapping a group card navigates to its episodes. The groups mode enables yearHeaderMode, showSeasonNumber, showDateRange, and customSort features.

yearHeaderMode.description: Controls how groups are organized under year headers in the group list view. Only meaningful when contentType is 'groups'. When omitted, defaults to 'none'. 'none': groups are displayed in a flat list without year headers. 'firstEpisode': each group appears once, placed under the year of its earliest episode; groups are organized under sticky year headers. 'perEpisode': a group appears multiple times, once for each year it contains episodes; tapping a group-year entry shows only that year's episodes from the group. When yearHeaderMode is not 'none', the sort order toggle is automatically shown in the UI.

episodeYearHeaders.description: Show year separator headers within episode lists. When true, episodes inside a playlist or group are visually separated by publication year with sticky headers. This controls year headers at the episode level (inside a group), as opposed to yearHeaderMode which controls year headers at the group level (the group list view). Can be overridden per-group via GroupDef.episodeYearHeaders.

showDateRange.description: Show date range and total duration metadata on group cards. When true, each group card displays the date span of its episodes (formatted as 'M/d - M/d' for same-year ranges or 'MMM d, yyyy - MMM d, yyyy' for cross-year ranges) and total playback duration (e.g., '3h 45m'). The date and duration values are computed at runtime from episode data. Can be overridden per-group via GroupDef.showDateRange.

showSortOrderToggle.description: Force the sort order toggle button to appear in the UI. The toggle is also shown automatically when yearHeaderMode is not 'none', regardless of this setting. When customSort is provided and this is true, the initial sort direction is derived from the first customSort rule's order; otherwise it defaults to descending. The toggle allows users to flip between ascending and descending order at runtime.

showSeasonNumber.description: Prepend a season number label to group display names. When true, groups with a sortKey greater than 0 are displayed as 'S{sortKey} {displayName}' (e.g., 'S13 Lincoln Arc'). Groups with sortKey 0 (typically special/ungrouped episodes) are displayed without the prefix. Applies to both the group card title and the playlist title itself.

titleFilter.description: Case-insensitive regex inclusion filter applied to episode titles before resolver processing. Only episodes whose title matches this pattern are passed to the resolver. Having any filter (titleFilter, excludeFilter, or requireFilter) makes this definition a 'filtered' definition, which is processed before filter-less fallback definitions. Matched episodes are claimed and unavailable to later definitions.

excludeFilter.description: Case-insensitive regex exclusion filter applied to episode titles. Episodes whose title matches this pattern are excluded, even if they passed titleFilter. Applied after titleFilter. Useful for carving out subsets: e.g., one definition uses titleFilter to match all numbered episodes, while excludeFilter removes a specific sub-series that another definition will claim.

requireFilter.description: Case-insensitive regex requirement filter applied to episode titles. Episodes must match this pattern to be included. Applied after titleFilter and excludeFilter. Semantically similar to titleFilter but intended for narrowing within an already-filtered set: e.g., titleFilter selects numbered episodes, requireFilter further restricts to a specific sub-series.

nullSeasonGroupKey.description: Group key assigned to episodes that have a null or zero seasonNumber in RSS metadata. Used only by the rss resolver. When set, episodes without a valid season number are placed into a group with this key (e.g., 0 for a 'Specials' group). When omitted, such episodes are left ungrouped and may fall through to a fallback definition.

groups.description: Static group definitions, primarily used by the category resolver. Each group defines a regex pattern for matching episode titles. Groups are evaluated in order; the first matching pattern claims each episode (first-match-wins). A group without a pattern acts as a catch-all for unmatched episodes and should be placed last. For the titleAppearanceOrder resolver, only groups[0].pattern is used as a fallback extraction pattern when titleExtractor is not provided.

customSort.description: Custom sort specification for ordering groups when contentType is 'groups'. Rules are evaluated in order; the first rule that produces a non-zero comparison decides the sort order. Conditional rules (with a condition field) are skipped when the condition is not met for both compared items. In 'episodes' mode, customSort only affects the initial sort direction when showSortOrderToggle is true (uses the first rule's order field); the actual group sorting logic has no effect since there are no groups.

titleExtractor.description: Configuration for extracting group display names from episode data at runtime. Used by rss, year, and titleAppearanceOrder resolvers to generate human-readable names for each group (e.g., 'Season 3' or 'Lincoln Arc'). Not used by the category resolver, which uses static display names from GroupDef entries. The extractor runs against a representative episode from each group (typically the first episode).

smartPlaylistEpisodeExtractor.description: Configuration for extracting season and episode numbers from episode title or description text. Overrides RSS metadata (itunes:season, itunes:episode) with values parsed from the episode content. Essential for podcasts that encode numbering in titles (e.g., '[62-15] Title') rather than using standard RSS tags. The extracted values are used for grouping (season) and ordering (episode number) within groups.

GroupDef.description: Static group definition for the category resolver. Groups with a pattern match episodes by title regex; a group without a pattern serves as a catch-all for unmatched episodes. Groups are evaluated in definition order (first-match-wins). At most one catch-all group should exist and should be placed last.

id.description: Unique identifier within the parent playlist definition. Used internally for mapping episodes to groups and for cache storage keys.

displayName.description: Human-readable group name shown on the group card in the UI. For the category resolver this is always used as-is (not overridden by titleExtractor).

pattern.description: Case-insensitive regex pattern matched against episode titles. When present, episodes whose title matches are assigned to this group. When absent (null), this group acts as a catch-all that collects all episodes not matched by any preceding group's pattern.

episodeYearHeaders.description: Per-group override for showing year separator headers within this group's episode list. When null (omitted), inherits the value from the parent playlist's episodeYearHeaders field.

showDateRange.description: Per-group override for showing date range and duration metadata on this group's card. When null (omitted), inherits the value from the parent playlist's showDateRange field.

SortSpec.description: Sort specification containing an ordered list of rules. Rules are evaluated sequentially; the first rule that produces a non-zero comparison determines the final order. If all rules produce zero (equal), the items retain their original order.

rules.description: Ordered list of sort rules. Must contain at least one rule. Earlier rules take precedence; later rules serve as tiebreakers.

SortRule.description: A single sort rule. When a condition is present, the rule is only applied if both compared items satisfy the condition; otherwise the rule is skipped and the next rule in the list is tried.

field.description: The group attribute to compare when sorting.

oneOf.description: Sort by the group's numeric sort key. For rss resolver, this is the season number. For year resolver, the publication year. For titleAppearanceOrder, the first-appearance index. For category, the group's position in the groups array.
Sort by the publication date of the most recent episode in each group. Groups with more recently published episodes sort first in descending order.
Sort by the user's listening progress percentage within each group. Computed at runtime on the mobile app from played/total episode counts. Has no effect in the web editor preview.
Sort alphabetically by the group's display name using locale-aware string comparison.

order.description: Sort direction. 'ascending': smallest/earliest/A-first values first. 'descending': largest/latest/Z-first values first.

condition.description: Optional condition that gates this rule. When present, this rule is only applied if BOTH compared groups satisfy the condition. If either group fails the condition, this rule is skipped entirely and evaluation falls through to the next rule.

SortCondition.description: Condition that gates a sort rule. The rule is only applied when both compared groups satisfy this condition. This enables two-tier sorting: e.g., sort numbered groups by number, but sort special groups (sortKey=0) by date.

type.description: Condition type. Both values have the same behavior: the condition is satisfied when the group's sortKey exceeds the threshold value. 'sortKeyGreaterThan' is the canonical name; 'greaterThan' is a legacy alias accepted for backward compatibility.

value.description: Threshold value. The condition is satisfied when the group's sortKey is strictly greater than this value. Example: value=0 means the rule applies only to groups with sortKey >= 1, excluding special groups (sortKey=0) from this sort rule.

TitleExtractor.description: Extracts a display name for a group from episode data. The extraction pipeline runs in this order: (1) if fallbackValue is set and source is 'seasonNumber' or 'episodeNumber' with a null/zero value, return fallbackValue immediately; (2) read the source field from the episode; (3) if pattern is set, apply regex and extract the specified capture group; (4) if template is set, substitute the extracted value into the {value} placeholder; (5) if any step produces null, try the fallback extractor recursively. This enables chained extraction strategies for complex podcast formats.

source.description: Episode field to read the raw value from. 'title': the episode title string. 'description': the episode description/summary string. 'seasonNumber': the episode's season number as a string (from RSS itunes:season or smartPlaylistEpisodeExtractor). 'episodeNumber': the episode number as a string (from RSS itunes:episode or smartPlaylistEpisodeExtractor). For 'seasonNumber' and 'episodeNumber', the numeric value is converted to a string before pattern matching.

pattern.description: Regex pattern applied to the source value. When provided, the first match is used and the capture group specified by 'group' is extracted. When omitted, the raw source value is used directly (useful for 'seasonNumber' source with a template like 'Season {value}').

group.description: Capture group index to extract from the regex match. 0 returns the full match. 1+ returns the specified capture group. Only meaningful when pattern is set.

template.description: Format template applied after extraction. The placeholder {value} is replaced with the extracted value. Example: 'Season {value}' with extracted value '3' produces 'Season 3'. Applied after pattern matching (or raw source value if no pattern).

fallback.description: Fallback extractor tried when this extractor fails to produce a value (source value is null, or pattern doesn't match). Supports arbitrary nesting for multi-tier fallback chains. Not tried when fallbackValue already returned a result.

fallbackValue.description: Static string returned immediately when source is 'seasonNumber' or 'episodeNumber' and the numeric value is null or less than 1. Checked before any pattern matching. Has no effect when source is 'title' or 'description'. Useful for labeling special/ungrouped episodes (e.g., 'Extras' for season 0).

EpisodeExtractor.description: Extracts season and episode numbers from episode text, overriding unreliable RSS metadata. Uses a three-tier fallback strategy: (1) primary pattern with separate capture groups for season and episode; (2) fallback pattern for special episodes with a fixed season number; (3) RSS episodeNumber as last resort. Essential for podcasts that encode numbering in titles like '[62-15] Title' rather than using standard RSS tags.

source.description: Episode field to extract numbers from. 'title': the episode title (most common). 'description': the episode description/summary.

pattern.description: Primary regex pattern with capture groups for season and episode numbers. Must contain at least one capture group for the episode number (episodeGroup). Should contain a capture group for season (seasonGroup) unless seasonGroup is null. Example: '[(\d+)-(\d+)]' captures season from group 1 and episode from group 2.

seasonGroup.description: Capture group index for the season number in the primary pattern. Default is 1. Set to null to skip season extraction entirely (episode-only mode), useful when only episode renumbering is needed without changing season grouping.

episodeGroup.description: Capture group index for the episode number in the primary pattern. Default is 2. The extracted value is parsed as an integer and used for episode ordering within groups.

fallbackSeasonNumber.description: Fixed season number assigned when the primary pattern fails but fallbackEpisodePattern matches. Typically 0 for special/bonus episodes that don't belong to a regular season. Only used in conjunction with fallbackEpisodePattern.

fallbackEpisodePattern.description: Secondary regex pattern tried when the primary pattern does not match. Designed for special episodes with a different title format (e.g., bonus episodes, extras). The episode number is extracted from the capture group specified by fallbackEpisodeCaptureGroup, and the season is set to fallbackSeasonNumber.

fallbackEpisodeCaptureGroup.description: Capture group index for the episode number in fallbackEpisodePattern. Default is 1.

fallbackToRss.description: When true, fall back to the episode's RSS episodeNumber (itunes:episode) if neither the primary pattern nor the fallback pattern matched. This is the last resort in the three-tier extraction strategy. Season number is not set in this case (only episode number is used).
